rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MCALLISTER et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Summary Adjudication By Defendant M. Slayen And Associates, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 22, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant M. Slayen and Associates, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issue 2 (loss of consortium) and Issue 3 (pulmonary injuries) and taken off calendar as to Issue 1 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issue 3, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff Thomas McAllister's claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Specifically, defendant demonstrated that Mr. McAllister was disabled in 2005 and that his disability was at least contributed to by his exposure to asbestos. However defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that he knew, or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, the cause or contributing factors of his disability more than one year before plaintiffs filed this action. As to summary adjudication Issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff Velma Jean McAllister's cause of action for loss of consortium is barred by the statute of limitations. Summary adjudication Issue 1 is taken off calendar because plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. The Court considered defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication. Plaintiffs' objections that it violates CRC 3.1350 are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
